supply_chain.dependencies: Raw materials include mild steel, stainless steel, electronic components (drives and motors), plastics (resins, fiberglass, epoxies), metals and paint. Certain commodities such as metals and resins are subject to commodity market and duty-driven price fluctuations.
supply_chain.risks: Supply chain challenges including increased lead times for raw materials due to availability constraints and high demand. Potential tariffs and reactionary tariff adjustments by other countries may contribute to inflationary cost increases. Interruption of supply chain could affect ability to produce or deliver products. Reliance on third-party suppliers and common carriers. Trade restrictions, embargoes, customs restrictions. Lack of availability of marine cargo insurance for shipments in certain geographies due to hostilities.

regulatory_risks: Changes in U.S. or foreign government administrative policy including imposition of or increases in tariffs
Prohibitions or restrictions on doing business with certain entities, including those with certain relationships with China
Compliance with U.S. Foreign Corrupt Practices Act (FCPA) and U.K. Bribery Act
International trade compliance regulations and export controls for dual use products
Environmental laws and regulations (EPA greenhouse gas emissions, state GHG programs)
Climate change regulations including Corporate Sustainability Reporting Directive (CSRD) in European Union
Data privacy laws including GDPR in European Union and California Consumer Privacy Act
Income tax law changes including OECD Pillar Two Model Rules for global 15% minimum tax
Product efficiency and performance standards, material makeup regulations, air quality and emissions standards
PFAS (perfluoroalkyl and polyfluoroalkyl substances) and other substances of concern regulations
Employment and labor regulations in various jurisdictions

sanction_risks: U.S. administration implementation of tariffs and potential additional tariffs
Sanctions on entities with relationships to China
Military conflicts between Russia and Ukraine and in the Middle East impacting economies
Trade war and governmental action related to tariffs or international trade agreements
International hostilities and military conflicts affecting operations, supply chains, and end-markets
